sections.text:
In accordance with the Digital Transformation Agency’s (DTA) Policy for responsible use of
AI in government, this page presents the Independent Health and Aged Care Pricing 
Authority’s statement on Artificial Intelligence Transparency.

sections.heading: How IHACPA uses Artificial Intelligence
sections.text: At this time, IHACPA does not use Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in its decisionmaking.

sections.heading: Monitoring and Governance
sections.text:
Accountable Officials:
IHACPA has designated Julia Hume, Executive Director, Costing and Data Infrastructure be 
the AI Accountable Officer.

sections.heading: Responsible AI usage policy:
sections.text:
IHACPA has developed and maintains a Policy for the responsible use of artificial
intelligence (AI). The policy aligns with advice and guidance provided by the DTA for using AI
services ethically and responsibly to ensure that it is appropriately governed, is safe and that
risks are identified and addressed.

sections.heading: Training and assistance:
sections.text:
All IHACPA staff will undertake the DTA’s AI fundamentals training modules. 
Additional training will be provided to staff in consideration of their roles and responsibilities, 
such as those responsible for the procurement, development, training and deployment of AI 
systems.

sections.heading: Compliance with Legislation and Regulations
sections.text:
IHACPA ensures AI usage complies with Australian legislation, such as data privacy and
anti-discrimination laws through the implementation of its Policy for the responsible use of
artificial intelligence (AI). The Policy aligns with the Australian Government's Policy for
responsible use of AI in government.

sections.heading: Statement Review and Updates
sections.text:
This transparency statement was last updated on 26 February 2025.
This statement will be updated as and when IHACPA’s approach to AI changes or when 
developments materially affect the statement’s accuracy, and at least every twelve months.

sections.heading: Contact Information
sections.text: For inquiries about IHACPA's AI use, please email: SecurityIHACPA@ihacpa.gov.au.
